lines: A tin ledger hangs at the stair-head on a nail, the warden's hand keeping count of the dead the way it kept count of everything else.

'Quarantine House Seventeen, the under-galleries. I lay them down by what the cart-papers called them, not by name. The sick west of the stair. The doubters across from them. The condemned below. The loud ones below that. It is easier to find a body when you know what it was sent here to be.'
'I had laid four hundred and some before I stopped keeping the running total. They came up the Hallowfen road faster than the cold could take them. I made room. A house keeps the dead it is given.'
